channel.title: Science - Latest - Google News
channel.copyright: Copyright © 2026 Google. All rights reserved. This XML feed is made available solely for the purpose of rendering Google News results within a personal feed reader for personal, non-commercial use. Any other use of the feed is expressly prohibited. By accessing this feed or using these results in any manner whatsoever, you agree to be bound by the foregoing restrictions.

item.title: Rare Blue Micromoon to rise this weekend - MLive.com
item.description:
1. Rare Blue Micromoon to rise this weekend  MLive.com
  2. How and when to watch May’s blue moon  CNN
  3. You may see the rare blue moon over the Philadelphia area this weekend  CBS News
  4. A Smaller Than Usual Blue Moon Will End the Month With a Lunar Spectacle. Here's What to Know About the Full Micromoon  Smithsonian Magazine
  5. Rare Blue Moon hides the red light of an ancient star on May 31 — Will you be able to see it?  Space

item.description:
1. Four ppm measurement of the antihydrogen ground-state hyperfine splitting  Nature
  2. Antihydrogen mirrors hydrogen in upgraded spectrum test, narrowing cosmic mystery  Phys.org

  4. ALPHA measures tiny energy gap in antimatter with improved precision – Home  Home | CERN
  5. Researchers in breakthrough precision on energy difference inside antihydrogen atoms  Swansea University

item.title: NASA's next major space telescope, the Nancy Grace Roman, is expected to find roughly 100,000 new transiting planets in just five years — along with the largest catalogue ever assembled of rogue worlds drifting through the galaxy without a star to orbit - Space Daily
item.description:
1. NASA's next major space telescope, the Nancy Grace Roman, is expected to find roughly 100,000 new transiting planets in just five years — along with the largest catalogue ever assembled of rogue worlds drifting through the galaxy without a star to orbit  Space Daily
  2. Peering into the Milky Way's far side, Roman could unveil 100,000 worlds  Phys.org
  3. NASA's Nancy Grace Roman telescope aims to unlock dark energy secrets  KOMO
  4. NASA's Roman Telescope Mirror Passes Final Review  Mirage News
  5. Moment of Science: NASA’s Roman Space Telescope set to revolutionize astronomy  WTVG

item.title: Pigeons navigate using magnetic sensors in livers — study - DW.com
item.description:
1. Pigeons navigate using magnetic sensors in livers — study  DW.com
  2. To travel far on cloudy days, pigeons listen to their gut  CNN
  3. How do pigeons sense Earth's magnetic field? Scientists say they’ve solved the mystery.  National Geographic
  4. A new study says homing pigeon livers act like compasses. Other experts aren’t so sure  Scientific American
  5. Pigeons may be navigating with their liver, study suggests  AP News

item.title: Severed sea cucumber appendages don’t seem to die - Ars Technica
item.description:
1. Severed sea cucumber appendages don’t seem to die  Ars Technica
  2. 'Immortal' tendencies observed in severed sea cucumber tissues  Interesting Engineering
  3. A severed piece of sea cucumber refused to die, and what happened next could transform medicine  Phys.org
  4. Can Cells from a Sea Cucumber Live Forever?  Nautilus | Science
  5. Sea cucumber tissue heals itself and survives for years after being detached from the body  Earth.com

item.title: James Webb Space Telescope discovers a black hole that formed before its host galaxy. Scientists aren't sure how - Space
item.description:
1. James Webb Space Telescope discovers a black hole that formed before its host galaxy. Scientists aren't sure how  Space
  2. A direct black-hole mass measurement in a little red dot at high redshift  Nature
  3. Little Red Dot Abell2744-QSO1: Sonification of Gas Velocity Around a Supermassive Black Hole (NIRCam and NIRSpec IFU)  NASA Science (.gov)
  4. Scientists Found a Black Hole That Breaks The Rules of Astrophysics  ScienceAlert
  5. 'A Paradigm Shift': Supermassive Black Hole Without a Galaxy Changes What We Thought Came First  Gizmodo

item.title: The asteroid that wiped out the dinosaurs may have struck a planet that was already in serious trouble — a new Johns Hopkins study has found evidence of a separate ecological crisis that began about 30,000 years before the impact, coincident with a high-vo - Space Daily
item.description:
1. The asteroid that wiped out the dinosaurs may have struck a planet that was already in serious trouble — a new Johns Hopkins study has found evidence of a separate ecological crisis that began about 30,000 years before the impact, coincident with a high-vo  Space Daily
  2. Fungi Bloomed Twice around End-Cretaceous Mass Extinction  Sci.News
  3. When The Dinosaur-Killing Asteroid Hit, This Life-Form Feasted On The Death  ScienceAlert
  4. Extinction: What were conditions like on Earth when the dinosaur-killing asteroid crashed into the planet?  FOX Weather
  5. Story of dinosaur-killer meteorite impact told in vivid detail  The Guardian

item.title: New evidence suggests Saturn's rings are younger than the dinosaurs — and for most of the planet's history, the Saturn you picture may never have existed - Space Daily

item.title: A pair of American satellites built to catch the Soviets cheating on a nuclear test ban kept detecting unexplained flashes, and the flashes turned out to be the most powerful explosions in the universe coming from billions of light-years away - Space Daily

item.title: Mount Everest is the highest point above sea level, but because the Earth bulges at the equator, the summit of a volcano in Ecuador sits farther from the centre of the planet, making it the closest piece of land on Earth to outer space. - Space Daily
